title: Gyárfás–Sumner Conjecture (Conjecture 1)
statement_text: For every integer $k$ and tree $T$, there is an integer $f(k, T)$ such that every graph with chromatic number at least $f(k, T)$ contains either a clique of size $k$, or an induced copy of $T$.
context_text: Gyárfás and Sumner independently conjectured that for any tree $T$, the class $\mathrm{Forb}(T)$ is $\chi$-bounded. This is described as a 'beautiful and difficult conjecture' and serves as the central motivation for the paper's extension to oriented graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves extensions to oriented graphs: for every oriented star $S$, every oriented graph with sufficiently large chromatic number contains either a transitive tournament $TT_3$ or $S$ as an induced subdigraph (Theorem 15), and establishes positive and negative $\chi$-boundedness results for classes of oriented graphs forbidding subsets of orientations of $P_4$.
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled. Gyárfás [14] published in 1975; Sumner [29] independently; 1975 used as the earlier date.

title: Conjecture 2
statement_text: $\mathrm{Forb}(H)$ is $\chi$-bounded if and only if $H$ is a forest.
context_text: The paper notes that an easy argument shows this conjecture is equivalent to the Gyárfás–Sumner Conjecture (Conjecture 1). It generalises the 'only if' direction (which follows from Erdős's girth theorem) to a complete characterisation.
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled. Stated by the paper authors as a reformulation equivalent to the Gyárfás–Sumner Conjecture; no separate citation in the header.

title: Conjecture 4
context_text: This is the paper's central new conjecture, extending the Gyárfás–Sumner framework to oriented graphs. The authors note it suffices to prove it for $S_{k,k}$ for all $k$, and prove several special cases: $k=0$, $k=\ell=1$, and the $TT_3$-free case (Theorem 15).
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled. Introduced with 'We conjecture the following'.

title: Conjecture 5
statement_text: Let $\mathcal{P}$ be a non-empty subset of $\mathrm{Or}(P_4)$. If $\mathcal{P} \neq \{P^+(3)\}$ and $\mathcal{P} \neq \{P^+(1,1,1)\}$, then $\mathrm{Forb}(\mathcal{P})$ is $\chi$-bounded.
context_text: Among the four non-isomorphic orientations of $P_4$, the paper shows $\mathrm{Forb}(P^+(3))$ and $\mathrm{Forb}(P^+(1,1,1))$ are not $\chi$-bounded. The conjecture asserts these are the only obstructions; the paper proves it when $P^+(3) \in \mathcal{P}$ (Corollary 35).
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled. Preceded by the informal statement 'We believe that $\{P^+(3)\}$ and $\{P^+(1,1,1)\}$ are the only non-empty subsets $\mathcal{P}$ of $\mathrm{Or}(P_4)$ such that $\mathrm{Forb}(\mathcal{P})$ is not $\chi$-bounded.'
